seoTitle: Mount Thorley vs Mount Vernon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Thorley and Mount Vernon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Thorley vs Mount Vernon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Thorley and Mount Vernon.
heading: Mount Thorley vs Mount Vernon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Thorley, New South Wales and Mount Vernon, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Thorley has a lower overall climate risk score (44/100) compared to Mount Vernon (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Thorley or Mount Vernon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Thorley has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Mount Vernon scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Thorley scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Vernon scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Thorley or Mount Vernon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Thorley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
